chapter_title.en: What Can and Cannot Be Used for Prostration

text: : مَرَّ بِي أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا أُصَلِّي عَلَى اَلطَّبَرِيِّ وَ قَدْ أَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْهِ شَيْئاً فَقَالَ لِي «مَا لَكَ لاَ تَسْجُدُ عَلَيْهِ أَ لَيْسَ هُوَ مِنْ نَبَاتِ اَلْأَرْضِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.831 - It was narrated from the Yāsir al-Khadim that He said:
"Abu al-Hasan (as) passed by me while I was praying on a piece of ṭabarī (a mat) upon which I had placed something.
Imam (as) said to me: 'Why do you not prostrate on it? Is it not from what the earth produces?'"
